BACK: the rear surface of the human body
BANISH: to send someone away as punishment
BANQUET: an elaborate formal meal for many people
BARGAIN: an agreement between parties; a good deal
BARRAGE: a concentrated artillery bombardment; heavy attack
BARRIER: a fence or obstacle that prevents movement
BARTER: to exchange goods without using money
BASE: the lowest part of something; foundation
BASIS: the underlying support for an idea
BATTLE: a fight between armies or opposing groups
BAY: a broad inlet of the sea
BEAM: a ray of light; a structural support
BEGIN: to start; perform the first part
BELIEF: an acceptance that something is true
BELIEVE: to accept that something is true
BESTOW: to confer or present as honor or gift
BETRAY: to expose someone to danger by treachery
BEVY: a large group of people or things
BIAS: prejudice in favor or against something
BITING: having a sharp, bitter taste; harsh
BITTERNESS: anger and disappointment at unfair treatment
BLEMISH: a small mark that spoils appearance
BLIZZARD: a severe snowstorm with high winds
BLOCK: a large solid piece; to obstruct
BLUFF: to try to deceive by false confidence
BLUR: to make unclear or less distinct
BODY: the physical structure of a person
BOND: a connection between people; to form connection
BORROW: to take temporarily with intention to return
BOUNDARY: a line marking limits of area
BRACE: to strengthen with support; prepare for impact
BRANCH: a part of tree growing from trunk
BREACH: an act of breaking rule or agreement
BREADTH: the distance from side to side; width
BREAK: to separate into pieces; to stop working
BREATHE: to take air into lungs and expel it
BREED: to produce offspring; a variety of animal
BRIEF: lasting only a short time; concise
BRING: to carry or accompany to a place
BRITTLE: hard but liable to break easily
BROADEN: to become wider; make more comprehensive
BRUSH: a tool with bristles; to clean lightly
BRUSQUE: abrupt or offhand in speech or manner
BUD: a compact growth on plant that develops
BUILD: to construct by putting parts together
BUMPY: having an uneven surface; rough ride
BURGEON: to begin to grow rapidly; flourish
BUOYANT: cheerful and optimistic; able to float
